text: In this work, we propose a new 3D object detector with reliable depth estimation, referred to as BEVDepth for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) 3D object detection. A primary motivation behind our work is that depth estimation in existing work is not very accurate even though depth information is required for 3D detection of camera. Our work, BEVDepth exploits the use of explicit depth supervision with a camera-awareness depth estimation module in order to enable an efficient depth prediction mechanism. We also exploit a Depth Refinement Module to eliminate side effects caused due to unprecise feature unprojection. Coupled with customized Efficient Voxel Pooling and multi-frame exploitation, our proposed 3D object detector achieves state-of-the-art results of 60.9% NDS on the widely adopted nuScenes test set while being very efficient in terms of time, thus for the first time the NDS score of a camera model reaches 60%. Codes have been made available.
